title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the ACTIVE engineering backlog (open + guardrail items; replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). Completed (Done) items are archived in data/backlog_archive.json to keep this file lean — the admin Backlog panel reads BOTH and merges them, so done items stay visible. The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay in the JSON.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.
donePolicy: When an item's status is set to `done`, delete its `prompt` field and keep only the `doneNote` — the doneNote is the shipped record of what was actually done, so the original prompt is redundant and only bloats this file (the admin backlog panel never renders done-item prompts anyway). Keep `prompt` on `open` and `guardrail` items, since those are still the actionable spec. Applies retroactively: a done item should never carry a prompt. RECURRING ITEMS: an item with `recurring: true` (e.g. R1 repo audit, CH16 staging→prod promotion) is a perpetual driver — NEVER set its status to done, set completedDate, or strip its prompt; it stays open across every run and its prompt leads with a RECURRING/REPEATABLE marker. Each run ships its output as new items, not by closing the recurring item. ARCHIVE (post-split): completed items now live in `data/backlog_archive.json`. When you set an item to `done` and strip its prompt, MOVE the item object out of backlog.json into backlog_archive.json (the admin panel merges both). Recurring items never move — they stay open in backlog.json.

items.title: Keep the Store interface as the only persistence seam
items.prompt: Guardrail for future work: all reads/writes stay behind `Store` (`src/lib/core/store.ts`) so the cloud-sync tier drops in a `CloudStore` with the same async methods. Reject any change that calls `indexedDB` directly from app/component code. AS BUILT (F63, 2026-07-06, staging-gated): the `CloudStore` (src/app/lib/cloudstore.ts) is now real and honors this seam — it is itself a StoreLike that wraps the local Store; App.svelte resolves the local Store via Entitlements.storeFor() and, on every non-demo surface (app + staging; cloud sync GA'd on prod CH16 2026-07-07), wraps it. The guardrail still holds unchanged: no component touches indexedDB or the network directly.

items.title: Second full repo audit
items.prompt:
RECURRING — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Each run is a fresh full audit (this is the Nth, not a one-shot); the findings ship as NEW backlog items, and R1 itself stays open for the next pass. Read-only audit (NO code edits) of the current repo — architecture, code quality, duplicated code, and security posture — accounting for the CURRENT architecture (ADR-001) AFTER the Svelte/TypeScript reorg. Specifically: a Vite multi-page build (vite.config.mjs + the vite-ssg.mjs plugin) to `dist/` (A26), served by Cloudflare Pages + `/functions/*` edge functions (TypeScript, A78). The source tree is `src/{lib,app,site}` (A30): src/lib/ is the framework-agnostic pure-logic core in native TS (A61) — core.ts (compute/cost/format/event bus/pure helpers), adapters.ts, store.ts, demostore.ts, curveseries.ts, report.ts, sampledata.ts, format.ts, types.ts — reused verbatim per A29; src/app/ is the Svelte 5 SPA on ALL app surfaces after the A33 cutover (app/demo/staging are marker-free mount points loading src/app/main.ts -> App.svelte; the sidebar-shell App.svelte + screens/ + parts/ (src/app/components/ was deleted in the CH16 cutover); app-only glue in src/app/lib/; the vanilla view layer was deleted); src/site/ is the marketing/info Svelte SSG (A69 — components prerendered by vite-ssg.mjs, hydrated in place). Constraints to verify against: runes-only Svelte 5 (no `export let`/`$:`/createEventDispatcher/svelte-store writables), `src/` is `any`-free TS, the mode-aware Store seam via context('bb:store') (real IndexedDB for app/staging, in-memory DemoStore for demo — demo NEVER persists or mutates), `style-src 'self'` CSP (S18/A55 — no inline styles), design tokens only in src/styles/tokens.css, .ts is linted (A79) and typecheck = tsc(core) + tsc(functions) + svelte-check(app), and two-track automated versioning (CH12, scripts/bump-version.mjs). THE hard product constraint: compute is local — no trade data ever leaves the browser. Give concrete, prioritized findings (P1/P2/P3 with file:line), and append any new actions to this backlog in the same format. Done when: a written, prioritized audit is delivered and new follow-ups are added to the backlog.

— UPDATED 2026-06-30 (canonical shadcn-svelte re-platform, ADR-002): audit against the CURRENT architecture — a conventional Svelte 5 (runes) + Tailwind v4 + canonical shadcn-svelte app. Tokens: the shadcn semantic set (background/foreground/card/primary/secondary/muted/accent/destructive/border/ring + chart-1..5) live in `src/styles/tailwind.css` (the single source; `tokens.css` is DELETED). UI primitives are canonical shadcn-svelte at `src/lib/components/ui/` (bits-ui v2, added via the CLI) — the old in-house `src/ui`/`$ui` are GONE. Structure: `$lib` → `src/lib`, with the pure-logic core at `src/lib/core/`, `cn` at `src/lib/utils.ts`. Most component styling is now Tailwind utilities (scoped `<style>` only for genuinely bespoke bits). See ADR-002.

— RUN 2026-07-01 (post-CH16 full audit): delivered. Repo in strong shape — NO P1. Verified CLEAN: no trade-data egress, CSP style-src 'self' holds (zero inline style=), functions auth hardened (constant-time compare + Access JWT + fail-closed), Stripe webhook inert+signature-verified, backup/screenshot restore sanitized (validShot/importAll), src/ any-free + runes-only, no cutover orphans/dangling imports, demo non-mutation holds (every dashboard write isDemo-guarded + DemoStore in-memory), Store seam only, version-bump/tsconfig/vite/playwright/eslint all current, no unused deps, no orphaned scripts. FIXED INLINE this run: shared money()/usd() from core across the 7 screens (had drifted); removed MOCK_* fixtures from the shipped screens + retired the redundant /dev/app.html RedesignApp harness (de-mocked, props required); disabled Trade Editor + CSV-import controls on demo (UI affordance to match the guards); doc/comment fixes (CLAUDE.md modal.ts + dev/screens + UI-workflow, core.ts orientation, screen 'on staging' comments, test-version.mjs stale path, ci.yml step name, .prettierignore/tsconfig.functions cosmetics). Deferred → CH38 (optional CI parallelism; renumbered from CH35 2026-07-05).

— RUN 2026-07-01 pass 5 (post-#92): delivered (docs/repo-audit-2026-07-01-pass5.md). Baseline green (tests/lint/typecheck/format/audit clean; bundle 517/600 KiB); moat verified intact (no egress, CSP holds, functions auth solid); all house-rule constraint greps clean. This pass audited feature-level behavior and found 1xP1 + 6xP2 user-visible functional bugs (mostly CH16 mock leftovers/lost seams): dead onboarding CSV CTA (A147), workspace-save-[] blanks dashboard (A148), Blotter dead write controls (A149), Calendar ISO-week mislabels (A150), zero-emitter event bus so Activity Terminal never populates anywhere (A151, corrects A133), missing Calendar lightbox dialog (A152), incomplete tag canonicalization/migration (A153). P3s: CSV-export formula hardening + importAll residuals (A154), verifyAccessJwt exp/iss (A155), Reports export ignores meta (A156), duplication batch (A157), dead-code prune (A158), unreachable tag filter (A159), CsvLibrary fake dropzone (A160), gate gaps (A161), untyped fetch boundaries (A162), docs drift (A163), #92 cosmetic regressions (A164).

— METHOD UPDATE 2026-07-01 (post pass 5): audit BEHAVIOR by default, not just static constraints. Pass 5 proved the hygiene gates (greps/typecheck/lint) catch zero functional bugs — all seven P1/P2s were controls that rendered but did nothing when clicked. Each future pass should (a) exercise every screen's visible controls (the no-dead-controls e2e sweep in e2e/no-dead-controls.spec.mjs automates the baseline — keep its allow-list honest and extend it to new screens/controls), (b) diff each screen's advertised affordances (copy, icons, buttons) against what the wiring actually does, and (c) verify any state written by one path is readable by every path that displays it (the A130/A153 tag-form class).

— RUN 2026-07-07 (post cloud-sync GA / A279 / A254 / A265; docs/repo-audit-2026-07-07.md): delivered via 4 parallel adversarially-verified dimension agents (architecture/duplication, Svelte5/TS + UI wiring, security/moat, correctness/tests/build). Baseline GREEN (npm test all 14 suites + build exit 0, no manifest drift, bundle 764/840 KiB). Verified CLEAN: the moat holds (only ciphertext + blinded ids + timestamps egress; workspace UUID not name; A254/A265 owner-only), CSP style-src 'self' (no inline style=), only {@html} sinks are a static SVG const, functions auth server-enforced + fail-closed (extends A277), runes-only + any-free, core math pinned by fixtures. Filed 12 findings A281–A292 and FIXED 10 of them THIS pass (shipped in the same PR): A281 client push-chunk cap 15→12 (live prod sync bug), A282 Reports CSV honors range, A283 shared feeForTrade, A284 syncPlan() + A279 direction tests, A285 buildReport est-comm footnote, A286 persist Calendar target, A287 drop dead drag-grip, A290 Analytics maxDD prop, A291 drop dead expiryLabel, A292 reconcileImport multiset keys. DEFERRED open (pure P3 dedup, no behavior bug, label-drift risk): A288 (costRowsFrom) + A289 (advStatRows). Also fixed inline: CLAUDE.md CH16-sweep leftovers (a 'staging only wraps' line + 2 stale F53 Account labels). Leave R1 OPEN (recurring).

— RUN 2026-07-07 PASS 2 (docs/repo-audit-2026-07-07-pass2.md): a five-DIMENSION deep audit via five parallel read-only agents (authentication, user-mgmt/accounts, vault+cloud-sync architecture, cloud-sync UX behaviour, project structure) with adversarial per-finding verification + cross-agent synthesis (2 findings independently corroborated by 2 agents). Went deeper than the perimeter (which held): the exposure is in the SYNC DATA MODEL, the RECOVERY story, and the SUBSCRIPTION lifecycle. Filed A297-A314 (18 items): 4 P1 (A297 pulled-trades re-stamped updated → re-push echo + delete-resurrection; A298 non-Default workspaces unreachable on device B + divergent duplicate DEK; A299 global-not-per-workspace status/pending/error + the pull-clears-pending A279 regression; A300 recovery unreachable behind the prod login gate = permanent lockout), 9 P2 (A301 rate-limit result discarded on email-send; A302 no passkey/session revocation; A303 subscription grace over-grant + no webhook ordering + sub-charge-as-donation; A304 first-writer-wins DEK; A305 account-deletion + FK cleanup; A306 sync error/lapsed/paused UX; A307 mergeRecords abort-blind switch race; A308 AAD metadata auth; A309 long-offline tombstone strand + erase-404 self-heal), 5 P3 (A310 auth hardening; A311 sync-UX/vault polish; A312 A279 test coverage; A313 docs drift; A314 repo hygiene). VERIFIED CLEAN (recorded): WebAuthn+session+admin crypto, envelope crypto (extractability/HKDF/Argon2id/IV/versioning), seq atomicity + R2/D1 consistency + push-cap lockstep, webhook signature+idempotency+past_due anchoring, tsconfig/ESLint/vite/CI coverage, demo isolation. Leave R1 OPEN (recurring).

— RUN 2026-07-08 (R1 pass 3 of the 07-07/08 sequence; docs/repo-audit-2026-07-08.md): four parallel adversarially-verified dimension agents (architecture/duplication, Svelte5/TS + UI behavior wiring, security/moat, correctness/tests/build/CI) + TWO documentation-accuracy verifiers sweeping every current-state doc, weighted toward the delta since 8972210 (the A271/A272 module-sizing rework, modlayout makeLayoutKit, Calendar viewport fill, A315 changelog-email rework). Baseline GREEN (npm test 17 suites + build + size-budget 785/840 KiB + check-deploy 14/14 + no manifest drift). NO P1; moat verified intact (no new egress, CSP style-src 'self' holds, only {@html} sinks are static SVG consts, functions auth sound, deps pinned, no secrets). Filed A317-A325 (9 items) and FIXED ALL NINE this same pass: A317 drag-resize span math (right-column modules could never reach Large; + pointercancel leak + rAF latest-event + fill preview parity), A318 failed changelog broadcast was ledgered+green (now 502 + no ledger on total failure, workflow warns on partial), A319 extracted the copy-pasted size controller into the shared modsize.svelte.ts + ModuleSizeMenu/ModuleResizeHandle parts (+ dropped Analytics' dead animate:flip), A320 single-sourced the module key+label tables in modlayout.ts + dedupe in migrate/validate, A321 rate limiter on notify-changelog, A322 range presets local-date + call-time now, A323 CI drift gate covers build-econ-events (made idempotent) + check-mermaid in npm run ci, A324 analytics-VM node coverage + the corner-drag staging e2e, A325 the 17-fix docs-accuracy batch across 12 files. Dropped in verification: phosphor-svelte-unused (deliberate A180 styleguide comparison). Verified-clean list recorded in the report so the next pass doesn't re-audit. Leave R1 OPEN (recurring).

— RUN 2026-07-08 PASS 2 (post-#133/#134 delta; docs/repo-audit-2026-07-08-pass2.md): four parallel adversarially-verified dimension agents weighted toward the A316/A293/A278/A276/A273 delta since 4917630. Baseline GREEN (npm test 18 suites + build + size-budget 800.3/840 KiB + check-deploy 18/18 + no generated-data drift). NO P1; moat intact — reclaim/admin/subscription attack surface verified sound end-to-end (256-bit single-use hashed reclaim tokens, enumeration-safe, workspace-owner 409; admin double-auth constant-time + bound params + no PII overexposure; webhook stays the only tier writer; no new egress/sinks/inline styles; deps pinned). Filed A326-A331: 3xP2 (A326 subscription error-shape breaks the SubscribeForm hosted-Checkout fallback + raw codes shown; A327 check-deploy canonical guard unquoted glob silently skips src/help/*.html; A328 site→app layering inversion — /account.html imports src/app modules, relocate to src/lib) + 3xP3 (A329 account glue dedupe deleteAccount/EMAIL_RE/fmtDate; A330 demo.html inherits the Stripe CSP relaxation via /app/*; A331 docs/copy batch — missing 0.70.0 changelog entry, stale How-To strings, push.ts grantsCloud comment). Verified-clean list recorded in the report (reclaim flow, admin endpoints, subscription create, A319/A320 landings, howto→help lockstep, 18-suite consistency, store seam/core purity). FIXED ALL SIX this same pass (archived with doneNotes): the error-convention flip also applied to /api/checkout, the account module + SubscribeForm relocated to the new shared src/lib/account/ (svelte-check-owned), LaunchGate's 2 extra regex duplicates caught beyond the audit list, and check-deploy gained the demo-CSP contract guard. Followed by the full docs-accuracy sweep in the same PR. Leave R1 OPEN (recurring).

items.title: Guardrail: verify Stripe webhook signature before provisioning
items.prompt: Guardrail for the payments webhook. As of F54 (2026-07-06) functions/api/webhook.ts is LIVE and provisions the donor flag — but ONLY after verifying the Stripe signature (HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW request body with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, constant-time compare, timestamp/replay window) and deduping by event id. This guardrail now protects that code: reject any change that provisions/credits an account or entitlement before the signature verification passes, and never let provisioning depend on the fail-open rate limiter (S22).

items.title: Promote Staging to Prod
items.prompt:
REPEATABLE — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Run to promote staging-only feature(s) onto prod + demo. Since the A33 cutover all three surfaces (app/demo/staging) are ONE Svelte SPA under src/app/ — there is no separate staging codebase to copy. Staging-only behavior lives behind `isStaging` / `PAGE_MODE === 'staging'` runtime guards INSIDE the shared components (the old STAGING_PAGE export is retired); promotion = removing those guards so the feature ships on every surface.

SCOPE: promote the feature(s) named in the request; if none are named, promote the full staging delta (staging is a proving ground — every feature is destined for prod + demo). Inventory with `grep -rn "isStaging\|PAGE_MODE === 'staging'" src/app src/lib` and CLASSIFY each hit: FEATURE gate (promote) vs ENVIRONMENT infra (leave) — the environment set (isolated IndexedDB + seed via SEEDED, the per-surface storage key namespacing MOD_KEY/TABS_KEY/WS_KEY, backup filename, env pill/version track, the onboarding gate, the key-gate in functions/_middleware.ts) stays put. List each feature + its gate(s) before editing.

PER FEATURE: (1) flip the gate to unconditional — e.g. `isStaging ? a : b` -> `a`, `{#if isStaging}` -> unconditional, `class:staging={isStaging}` + `.overlay.staging` CSS -> `.overlay`; remove the now-unused STAGING_PAGE import. (2) DEMO 1:1 — the feature must work on demo, but keep demo NON-MUTATING: any TRADE-writing control stays `disabled` under `isDemo` and guarded (`if (isDemo) return;`), and the in-memory DemoStore means UI/layout prefs (saved layouts, hidden modules) simply don't persist — that's correct, not a bug. (3) keep it CSP-clean (no inline style="" — A55) and a11y-intact.

THEN: `npm test` (lint+typecheck+format+unit) + `npm run test:e2e` (boots all surfaces). UPDATE e2e: any spec asserting demo/prod LACKS the feature (e.g. 'demo … no menu', 'demo … not paginated') must flip to assert it now HAS it. Verify in a browser (build -> serve dist/ -> Playwright) that the demo + app surfaces show the feature. Title the PR `feat:` (or `fix:`) so CH12 bumps prod by the right level — do NOT hand-edit data/versions.json (staging keeps its lead). Add a data/changelog.json (F13) entry at the top of `releases`, keyed to the NEW prod version, describing what shipped for users. The bump-version STAGING_ONLY set is just `src/app/staging.html` (the env page) — features were never in it, so no change there.

Each run done when: the in-scope features are live on prod + demo at 1:1 (demo non-mutating), all surfaces verified, npm test + e2e green, a changelog entry added, committed/pushed (PR only if asked). Leave this item OPEN (repeatable).

— UPDATED 2026-07-01 (UI-redesign Phase 3 cutover): the promotion model above (flip STAGING_PAGE guards inside the shared App.svelte components) describes the PRE-redesign world and now applies ONLY to the small set of legacy staging features still gated that way (grep `STAGING_PAGE src/` — Overview/TradeBlotter/AdvancedStats). The DOMINANT staging delta is now the WHOLE redesigned sidebar-shell app: `src/app/main.ts` dynamically mounts `src/app/StagingApp.svelte` (+ `src/app/screens/*`, `src/app/lib/dashboard.svelte.ts`, `src/lib/components/shell/*`) for `PAGE_MODE==='staging'` ONLY, while app/demo still mount the legacy `src/app/App.svelte`. So the big promotion is a CUTOVER — point app/demo at the redesigned root too (drop the `PAGE_MODE==='staging'` branch in main.ts and retire App.svelte + its now-unreachable STAGING_PAGE branches) — NOT a per-gate flip. That cutover is a large, owner-gated milestone; scope it explicitly and confirm demo stays non-mutating (DemoStore) before running it. The env infra (isolated IndexedDB, seed, LS namespacing, _middleware key-gate) still keys off PAGE_MODE and stays put.

— UPDATED 2026-07-01 (the big cutover ran): this run promoted the ENTIRE redesigned sidebar-shell app to prod + demo. The legacy `src/app/App.svelte` + its whole `src/app/components/*` view layer (19 components) and the `src/app/lib/modules.ts` A108 module registry were RETIRED (deleted); the redesigned `src/app/StagingApp.svelte` was RENAMED to `src/app/App.svelte` and is now THE single mode-aware app root; `src/app/main.ts` mounts it on ALL surfaces (no more staging-only dynamic-import branch), mode-aware via PAGE_MODE (app → real IndexedDB, no seed, empty → first-run onboarding; demo → seeded in-memory DemoStore, every write isDemo-guarded so demo never persists; staging → isolated seeded DB). The `STAGING_PAGE` export was removed from core.ts (the app derives an `isStaging` local from PAGE_MODE). Full prod parity was built on the redesigned screens/parts: editable cost setup, first-run onboarding, saved views, workspace layouts, per-trade screenshots, day-note tags, backup/restore/erase, activity terminal, definitions, and status banners — with demo kept non-mutating throughout. Docs synced (ui-redesign.md/CLAUDE.md/architecture.md). Remaining recurring scope for future runs: promote any genuinely staging-only feature added AFTER this cutover. — INVENTORY 2026-07-01: the current staging-only feature awaiting promotion is A135 (tabbed dashboards, gated behind isStaging in App.svelte + DashTabs.svelte). Promote once proven on staging. — INVENTORY 2026-07-05 (backlog review): A135 was promoted 2026-07-02 (PR #95, 'dashboard tabs everywhere'). A fresh grep of src/ finds NO staging-only feature gates remaining — every isStaging/PAGE_MODE==='staging' hit is environment infra (isolated DB/seed, storage-key namespacing, env pill, middleware key-gate). Nothing currently awaits promotion; run again when the next staging-gated feature lands. — GATING MODEL (A111 decision, docs/staging-gating-recommendation.md): runtime PAGE_MODE/isStaging gating IS the staging model (one bundle, promotion = deleting the gate). Staging gates are UX switches, NOT security boundaries — anything genuinely private belongs behind the server-side middleware. When a staging-only feature exceeds ~10 KiB minified or pulls a new dependency, load it behind {#if isStaging}{#await import(...)} so it stays out of the prod boot payload. — INVENTORY 2026-07-05 (post-F53): ONE staging-gated feature awaits promotion — the F53 Account sidebar item + screen (gated on isStaging in App.svelte's sections/route). Promote ONLY after the owner completes the D1 setup (wrangler d1 create blotterbook-accounts + schema.sql + the ACCOUNTS_DB Pages binding) and phase 1 is proven on staging. — RUN 2026-07-07 (cloud sync GA on prod): promoted the cloud-sync feature (F58–F63 engine + the A279 sync UX) to prod. FINDING: there was NO isStaging code gate on cloud sync to flip — A256 already wrapped every non-demo Store in the CloudStore and configureCloudSync() already ran on prod (cloudSync.configured true on prod), so the Account cloud-sync card + WorkspaceSwitcher sync row + the A279 parity panel already rendered on prod, inert until a cloud-tier user opts in + unlocks. The promotion was therefore a GA DECLARATION + doc/comment alignment, not a gate flip: updated CLAUDE.md (intro + egress hard-constraint + the 'on staging only wraps' line + the sync-branch paragraph + repo-layout labels), docs/synced-workspaces.md (the stale 'STAGING-GATED / only on staging' F63 section — was factually wrong vs A256), the stale 'staging only' comments in cloudsync.svelte.ts (module header, wrapStore, configureCloudSync, cloudSync.configured) + Account.svelte/WorkspaceSwitcher/cloud-sync-ux-a279 doc; added a data/changelog.json 0.66.0 entry; extended the prod e2e (cloud-sync.spec.mjs test 5) to complete setup+unlock on /app/app.html and assert the parity panel + explainer render on prod (was card-only). Demo non-mutation unchanged (DemoStore is never wrapped). npm test + full e2e (101) green. INFRA (owner, confirmed done): prod Pages project has ACCOUNTS_DB (D1) + SYNC_BUCKET (R2) bound, schema applied, and a cloud subscription — without those /api/sync + /api/account 503 and sync stays unreachable regardless of the client code. INVENTORY 2026-07-07: a fresh grep of isStaging/PAGE_MODE==='staging' in src/app finds ONLY environment infra (LS namespacing MOD_KEY/TABS_KEY/WS_KEY/ECON_KEY, SEEDED seed, BACKUP_NAME, envLabel, appVersion, freshApp/needsOnboarding) + the F56 LaunchGate isStaging note — NO feature gate awaits promotion. Run again when the next staging-gated feature lands. — RUN 2026-07-08 (corner drag-resize promotion): promoted the A271/A319 corner drag-resize handle (the one feature gate in the 2026-07-07 inventory's successor state) to prod + demo. Flipped the two {#if isStaging} gates around ModuleResizeHandle in Dashboard.svelte + Analytics.svelte to unconditional and removed the now-unused isStaging prop from both screens + their App.svelte call sites (the screens carry no other staging dependence). Demo non-mutating by construction: the handle only stages layout prefs (DemoStore.local no-ops). e2e: added the promoted keyboard-resize + never-persists demo test (interactions.spec.mjs) and allow-listed the role=slider handle in the no-dead-controls sweep (a bare click is a no-op by design); the staging drag-engine tests (A317) unchanged. Changelog 0.69.0 entry added (shared with the A316 reclaim flow shipping in the same feat: PR). INVENTORY 2026-07-08: a fresh grep of isStaging/PAGE_MODE==='staging' in src/app finds ONLY environment infra (SEEDED seed, LS namespacing MOD_KEY/TABS_KEY/WS_KEY/ECON_KEY/CAL_TARGET_KEY/ANALYTICS_MOD_KEY, BACKUP_NAME, env pill/appVersion, freshApp/needsOnboarding, F56 gate arming) — NO feature gate awaits promotion. Run again when the next staging-gated feature lands. — RUN 2026-07-08 (Small/KPI slice, same-day): promoted the A271-remainder Small/KPI slice to prod + demo on the owner's instruction (same-day promotion, explicitly ordered). Flips: Dashboard.svelte's sizeKit wrapper (supportedSizes sm filter) reverted to the plain dashboardKit, pickerModules KPI filter removed (picker iterates MODULES again), the isStaging prop dropped from Dashboard Props + the App.svelte call site; modlayout.ts/CLAUDE.md comments updated. Demo non-mutating by construction (layout prefs live in DemoStore.local). e2e: the demo staging-gate test FLIPPED to a promoted test (picker offers the KPI cards, Win Rate renders a real computed headline at span-2, Size menu offers Small, reload reverts); the staging six-Small carousel test unchanged (still valid). INVENTORY 2026-07-08 (post-flip): only environment infra remains — NO feature gate awaits promotion. Run again when the next staging-gated feature lands.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Rate-data scraper must stay within the 50-subrequest free-tier cap
items.prompt: Guardrail for the roadmap's scheduled rate-data scraper (the Cron Worker that will pull CME fee schedules, per-broker data-feed prices, and per-state tax tables into `data/*.json`). The Workers FREE plan allows only 50 EXTERNAL subrequests per invocation. When this is built, design within that cap: batch/limit outbound fetches to <=50 per run (count the GitHub commit-back call), split sources across multiple scheduled invocations, or move to Workers Paid. Reject a single-invocation design that fans out to dozens of sources on the free tier.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Move to Workers Paid before shipping the paid cloud-sync tier
items.prompt: Guardrail for the planned cloud-sync subscription tier (the `CloudStore` behind the `Store` seam, A4). Every device push/pull is a Pages Function invocation against the Workers FREE plan's 100,000 requests/day cap — a few hundred active sync users would exhaust it — and KV's 1,000 writes/day free cap is far too low for user data. Do NOT ship a paying sync product on the free Workers plan: treat the Workers Paid plan ($5/mo — 10M requests + higher KV/CPU) as a prerequisite, with R2 (10 GB free, free egress — fits the zero-knowledge model) for encrypted trade blobs and D1/KV for accounts/entitlements. Plan the economics around Workers Paid (trivial against subscription revenue), not around stretching the free tier. Reject a sync rollout that assumes the free request/KV caps.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Keep Workers KV low-write — never store per-visitor state in KV
items.prompt: Guardrail for KV usage (`STATUS_KV`). The Workers FREE plan caps KV at 1,000 WRITES/day (plus 1,000 deletes + 1,000 lists; reads are 100k/day; 1 GB storage). Today KV holds only admin-set status + feature-flag config and the best-effort admin rate-limiter — all low-write and fail-open, which is fine. Keep it that way: never put per-VISITOR or per-REQUEST state in KV (counters, analytics, sessions, public-endpoint rate-limiting) — that would blow 1,000 writes/day almost immediately and start erroring. New per-user/per-request persistence belongs in D1/R2 (and on Workers Paid). Reject a change that writes to KV on a public or high-traffic path.

items.title: Guardrail: admin-endpoint security must not depend on the fail-open rate limiter
items.prompt: From the R1 audit. rateLimited() (functions/_lib/http.ts) returns false (ALLOW) whenever STATUS_KV is unbound and is best-effort/non-atomic; it is the only throttle on /api/status, /api/config, and /api/admin-key (token issuance, 20/60s). This is the correct trade-off (don't lock the admin out; endpoints sit behind Access + constant-time token/key compare), so it is defense-in-depth only. Guardrail: never let an endpoint's security DEPEND on rateLimited() — auth (Access JWT + constant-time compare) is the real control; reject any future endpoint that relies on the limiter as primary protection, and keep STATUS_KV bound in prod so the throttle is actually active. Adjacent: A17 (KV low-write), S11 (webhook signature).

items.title: (guardrail) Dev tooling stays dev-only — SUPERSEDED IN PART by ADR-001/A28
items.prompt: PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md. This guardrail was written for the R19 Tier A world where the shipped app was zero-dep and the committed files were the deploy artifact (so devDeps could never touch shipped bytes, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL=1 was set on Pages). ADR-001 reverses that: Tier B (Vite build) is being adopted, so build deps now DO produce shipped bytes and Pages WILL install + build. The replaced clauses: 'shipped output stays dependency-free', the drift gate as proof-of-no-shipped-deps, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL — those no longer hold after A26 lands. WHAT STILL APPLIES until/after migration: keep node_modules + build output (playwright-report, test-results, the Vite dist/output dir) gitignored; pin every dep with a committed lockfile. The NEW dependency discipline lives in A28 (minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain as a security control; local-compute pillar gates every dep). Reference A28, not this item, going forward.

items.title: (guardrail) Dependency policy — pinned/lockfiled/audited supply-chain hygiene (minimalism retired, ADR-002)
items.prompt:
Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md), replacing the zero-runtime-dep stance (A25). Runtime/build dependencies are now ALLOWED, under discipline — because for a privacy product handling financial data, the supply chain IS a security control. (1) EARN-ITS-WEIGHT: add a dep only when hand-rolling it is materially worse; keep writing small utilities by hand (small helpers). Heavyweight enablers qualify (the framework, charting like lightweight-charts, layout/virtualization libs); date/utility micro-libs do not. (2) SUPPLY CHAIN: pin every version + commit the lockfile; run `npm audit` in CI; apply Subresource Integrity / strict CSP to shipped bundles; keep the dependency surface minimal; consider vendoring the few critical libs so a registry compromise can't silently reach users. (3) LOCAL-COMPUTE PILLAR GATES EVERY DEP (hard): reject any dep or dep feature that phones home, ships telemetry/analytics, or could exfiltrate trade data — no exceptions, regardless of convenience. Reject any dependency addition that fails the earn-its-weight bar or the local-compute gate.

— UPDATED 2026-06-30 (canonical shadcn-svelte re-platform, ADR-002): the decision owner RETIRED the dependency-minimalism / earn-its-weight posture — Blotterbook now adopts dependencies freely as a conventional Svelte 5 + Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte app (the shadcn-svelte CLI is the component workflow; bits-ui/tailwind-variants/tw-animate-css/etc. ship). What REMAINS as a guardrail is supply-chain HYGIENE only: pin versions + commit the lockfile + `npm audit` in CI + keep CSP `style-src/script-src 'self'`. Drop the "minimal / no-runtime-dep / hand-roll it" language when citing A28.

items.title: (guardrail) Preserve the pure-logic core verbatim through the framework migration
items.prompt:
Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). The Svelte migration (A27 + later Phase 4) must touch the VIEW LAYER ONLY. The pure-logic modules are the hard-won, test-covered value and must be imported into components VERBATIM, not rewritten: src/lib/core/adapters.ts (platform CSV adapters + detection + pairFills), compute() (metrics), costModel() (commissions/subscriptions/tax/take-home), the Section-1256 tax model, src/lib/core/store.ts (the Store seam — A4), and src/lib/core/format.ts. They stay framework-agnostic so the node test suites (test-adapters/test-version/test-auth/test-flags/test-tax) keep covering them unchanged. Reject a framework PR that reimplements adapter parsing, the metrics/cost/tax math, or persistence inside a component instead of importing the existing module. The Store interface (A4) remains the only persistence seam. TYPESCRIPT CLARIFICATION (A61, docs/archive/typescript-decision.md): converting the core from JSDoc-typed .js to native .ts (folded into the A30 reorg) is PERMITTED — it changes the syntax host (.js→.ts, JSDoc→inline types), NOT the algorithms. 'Verbatim' here means NO logic rewrite, not 'never touch the file'. The gate is that the node suites pass IDENTICALLY before/after the conversion (they are the behavior-preserving safety net). Still reject any change that reimplements the parsing/metrics/cost/tax/persistence logic under cover of the TS migration.

— UPDATED 2026-06-30 (canonical shadcn-svelte re-platform, ADR-002): the Svelte/TS migration is COMPLETE and the canonical-shadcn re-platform moved the core to `src/lib/core/*.ts` (adapters/core/store/types/format/report/sampledata/demostore/curveseries). The guardrail still holds: the core stays framework-agnostic + node-tested (test-adapters/version/auth/flags/tax/demostore), imported by components as-is — never reimplement adapter parsing or the metrics/cost/tax math in a component.

items.title: Verify the 9 beta CSV adapters against real exports
items.prompt: Spun off from the R1 audit (2026-06-29); refreshed 2026-07-06. VERIFIED against real exports (beta:false): tradingview, tradingview-orders, tradovate + tradovate-perf + tradovate-fills (Tradovate/NinjaTrader family, incl. the platform's own Performance ground truth), quantower, and atas (ATAS X .xlsx). STILL BETA — need a real export each: motivewave, rithmic, sierrachart, tradestation, webull, ibkr, schwab. For each remaining beta adapter validate symbol/root, side, qty, PnL, date/time, and (for fills exports) the FIFO round-trip pairing + holdMs against a REAL statement, add a representative fixture to scripts/test-adapters.mjs, drop the beta flag, and note it in data/changelog.json. Needs real statements (external dependency); ship one adapter per pass. Done when the remaining beta set is validated (or the item is re-scoped as adapters are dropped).

items.title: How-to: add per-platform CSV-export screenshots + clearer steps
items.prompt: Improve the How-To wiki (src/site/components/Howto.svelte) so a new user can reliably export the right CSV from each supported platform. Add per-platform screenshots of the export flow and tighten the step-by-step instructions (where the export lives, which option to pick, common gotchas). Images go through Vite's asset pipeline (src/assets/ or co-located, fingerprinted) and must respect the CSP/img-src; keep the page prerendered (SSG, A69) and the bundle lean (A28/size-budget). Done when: each supported platform has clear instructions + at least one screenshot, and the page builds + prerenders cleanly. — UPDATE 2026-07-04: owner will capture screenshots; in the interim, use other trading-journal products' public import docs/wikis as REFERENCE for accurate per-platform steps, but do NOT republish their screenshots (copyright) — ship only our own captures or officially-licensed platform doc images.

items.title: Rates upkeep: verify 2026 CME fee changes in our composites + opportunistic history backfill
items.prompt: Follow-up from F30 (see docs/rates-data-assessment.md — Follow-ups). (1) CME published a 2026-02-01 fee schedule AND announced further transaction-fee changes effective 2026-04-01; our current composites in static/data/exchange-fees.json are a 'mid-2026 snapshot' (A171, 2026-07-02) that SHOULD include both — spot-check the equity/energy/metals/micro groups against cmegroup.com's current schedule (clearing-fees page); if anything moved, bump the current values and push the prior values into a new history entry ('until': '2026-03-31' or '2026-01-31' as documented), then run build-manifest.mjs. (2) Backfill deeper (pre-2024) exchange-fee history from cmegroup.com/company/clearing-fees/historical-fees.html only if users import multi-year-old data. (3) Add broker rateHistory entries ONLY with a citable source (broker announcement or a Wayback capture of their pricing page) — never fabricate. Schema needs no changes for any of this. Done when: the 2026 spot-check is recorded (values confirmed or corrected+dated) and any added history entries carry citations. PROGRESS (2026-07-06): verification attempted but the sandbox egress policy blocked cmegroup.com/broker pages (WebFetch/curl 403 on all hosts); via search snippets: Schwab/IBKR/Tradovate/Optimus/TradeStation commissions confirmed unchanged, no evidence of a 2026 retail base-fee hike (CME's Apr-2026 changes are member volume-incentive programs), EdgeClear std + AMP tier unverifiable. No data edited. Re-run with direct web access targeting cmegroup.com's 2026-02-01/04-01 PDFs + AMP's 2026 notice. PROGRESS (2026-07-06, direct web access): re-ran with working egress. cmegroup.com PDFs (incl. cme-fee-schedule-2026-02-01.pdf) time out in the fetcher (too large), but convergent evidence says our mid-2026 composites are STILL CURRENT and need no edit: (a) AMP Futures publishes a CME fee-increase notice every year a retail change lands (2023/2024/2025) and has NO 2026 notice — latest is 2025-02-01; (b) TradeStation's current non-member fee mirror still shows 2024-era base exchange fees (ES/NQ $1.38), i.e. no equity/energy/metals move; (c) CME re-dates the schedule PDF every February regardless of changes, so the 2026-02-01 file existing is not a change signal; (d) the 2026-04-01 CME change is member volume-incentive programs, not retail per-contract base fees. Deliberately did NOT reconcile from TradeStation numbers (their CL/MGC use a different exchange+clearing+NFA basis than our composites). NO data edited. Spot-check RECORDED = confirmed unchanged. Item stays open only for optional byte-level confirmation against the authoritative 2026-02-01/04-01 CME PDFs once a fetcher can handle them.

items.title: Backfill econ-calendar CPI/NFP 2021-2023 + GDP 2021, reconcile unverified 2025 rows
items.prompt:
Follow-up from F43 (econ-event calendar). The v1 static/data/econ-events.json shipped with gaps because bls.gov and the St. Louis Fed hosts were egress-blocked at build time: (a) CPI & NFP release dates for 2021, 2022, 2023 were OMITTED entirely (no marker on those days), (b) BEA GDP 2021 was omitted (the early-2021 archive page rendered ambiguously and the builder refused to guess), and (c) the 2025 CPI/NFP rows ARE included but UNVERIFIED — 2025 had a ~43-day government shutdown (Oct-mid-Nov) that cancelled the Oct NFP, pushed Sep NFP to 2025-11-20 and released Oct+Nov together 2025-12-16, so those rows need reconciliation against the authoritative BLS archive. When bls.gov (release-schedule archives) and bea.gov are reachable: transcribe the real CPI + NFP release dates for 2021-2023, backfill GDP 2021, and reconcile the whole 2025 CPI/NFP block against the BLS archive — editing the constant lists in scripts/build-econ-events.mjs (do NOT fabricate dates; omit anything you cannot verify), then re-run the generator + node scripts/build-manifest.mjs and confirm scripts/test-econ.mjs stays green. FOMC (2021-2026), GDP (2022-2026) and EIA are already verified — do not touch them. Done when: the omitted years are filled from citable primary-source dates, the 2025 rows are confirmed-or-corrected, the generator header documents the sourcing, and gates (incl. manifest drift) are green.

— BLOCKED ATTEMPT 2026-07-07: retried in a Claude Code web session; WebFetch was non-functional session-wide (HTTP 403 for every host incl. control URLs example.com/anthropic.com) and the egress proxy denied CONNECT to bls.gov/bea.gov/web.archive.org. WebSearch returned only snippet-level summaries (one internally inconsistent) — not primary-source page content — so NO dates were transcribed and NO edits were made (per the no-fabrication rule). Needs a session with working direct web access to the BLS/BEA schedule archives, or owner transcription.

items.title: Guardrail: accounts hold identity + entitlements ONLY — never trade data
items.prompt: Guardrail from R24. REFINED 2026-07-06 by synced workspaces (F58–F63; design + owner approval in docs/synced-workspaces.md): the server may now hold synced trade/journal/meta records — but ONLY as end-to-end-encrypted ciphertext it cannot decrypt (E2E, zero-knowledge). So the invariant becomes: NO server-READABLE (plaintext) trade, journal, layout, filter, or workspace-name data in D1/KV/R2 — ever. The account system stores identity + entitlements + opaque wrapped keys + ciphertext blobs (R2 SYNC_BUCKET) + a blinded-id change-index (D1); keys live in memory client-side only and the server never sees a symbol/P&L/note/tag/name. Any FURTHER widening of what leaves the browser still needs an explicit owner-approved decision (the A4 seam / a CloudStore ADR is where it lands). Auth security never depends on the fail-open rate limiter (extends S22): the real controls are the WebAuthn ceremony, single-use TTL'd challenges/recovery tokens, hashed session secrets, and Origin checks. Reject any change that writes PLAINTEXT/server-readable user trading data server-side, that sends any plaintext trade field to /functions, or that leans on rateLimited() as the primary protection.

items.title: Symbols dashboard — per-symbol info cards with expandable detail view
items.prompt: From the owner. A new 'Symbols' screen/dashboard showing an info card for every symbol the user has traded (derived from imported data). Each card: the symbol root, full contract name, current price (updates realtime — NOTE: requires a market-data source; the app is local-only with no egress for trade data, so a public quote API needs an ADR-level decision — evaluate free delayed-quote options and flag the privacy/CSP implications before wiring), the exchange it trades on (CME/CBOT/NYMEX/COMEX from exchange-fees.json), which platforms the user traded it on (file provenance via platformOf), the user's total PnL for that symbol, and which contract months were traded rendered in month-letter format (F/G/H/J/K/M/N/Q/U/V/X/Z — build on F40's expiry parse helper). Clicking a card expands a full-horizontal-width detail module with a per-symbol equity/PnL graph and expanded stats (win rate, trade count, avg win/loss, hold time if fills data). Reuse core compute + curveseries; new screen in src/app/screens/ + nav entry. Done when the screen renders cards for every traded root with accurate stats and the expanded view works on all surfaces (demo included, read-only).

items.title: (discuss + build) Quantower-style scrolling ticker tape in the app header (traded symbols, real-time prices)
items.prompt: Add a Quantower/TradingView-style horizontal scrolling TICKER TAPE to the app header, populated from the DISTINCT roots in the user's imported trades (unique roots from dash.allTrades), each chip showing symbol + last price + change, looping horizontally. ARCHITECT FIRST — the app has no market-data dependency today, so live prices are a NEW external network dependency: assess + document (a) a quote source that actually covers CME FUTURES roots (ES/NQ/CL/GC + micros; continuous-contract mapping; delayed vs realtime; cost/licensing; CORS/browser reachability), (b) transport (websocket stream vs debounced REST poll) + a shared cache + rate budget, (c) client-fetch vs a thin /functions key-hiding proxy. MOAT NOTE: price data is not trade data (no trade field egresses), but it is a new OUTBOUND call — gate it behind a user preference, DEFAULT OFF, so the zero-egress default holds; add the quote host to connect-src in static/_headers (owner-approved, minimal). Deliver the provider/transport assessment (docs/) BEFORE building. Build CSP-clean, no layout shift, pause when the tab is hidden, degrade gracefully when the feed is down (show last-known / hide prices). Staging-gate first. Done when: the assessment is recorded; the ticker renders the user's roots with live-ish prices behind a default-off preference; _headers/CSP updated minimally; offline/feed-down degrades cleanly.

items.title: (post-beta) Executive-level business overview document for corporate leads
items.prompt: From the owner. Write an executive-level BUSINESS document (docs/, e.g. docs/executive-overview.md — a polished, decision-oriented overview for a non-technical corporate audience) covering the platform end to end: what Blotterbook is + its core features/functionality (import → local compute → performance/cost/tax/calendar/analytics/reports), the SECURITY posture (local-compute moat — trade data never leaves the browser except opt-in E2E zero-knowledge cloud sync; CSP hardening; WebAuthn/passkey auth; server-side auth/entitlement enforcement), DATA MANAGEMENT (local-first IndexedDB, ciphertext-only egress, per-user storage quotas, delete/erase + retention), USER MANAGEMENT (accounts, passkeys, recovery, tiers/entitlements local vs cloud), PAYMENT HANDLING (Stripe subscription for the cloud tier + donations, PCI posture), a high-level architecture summary (Cloudflare Pages + edge functions + D1/R2), and the roadmap/positioning. Audience: potential corporate leads/partners in future meetings — accurate to the SHIPPED product, benefits-led, minimal jargon. BLOCKED — WAIT UNTIL OUT OF BETA (per owner): the product is in beta now; hold this until beta ends so the doc reflects the GA state (features, pricing, guarantees) rather than a moving target. Done when: the exec overview is written, accurate to the GA product, and owner-reviewed.

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
